dataformat.name: soap
dataformat.title: SOAP
dataformat.description: Marshal Java objects to SOAP messages and back.
dataformat.label: dataformat,transformation,xml

contextPath.description: Package name where your JAXB classes are located.

encoding.description: To overrule and use a specific encoding.

elementNameStrategy.label: advanced
elementNameStrategy.description: Refers to an element strategy to lookup from the registry.

version.description: SOAP version should either be 1.1 or 1.2. Is by default 1.1.

namespacePrefix.label: advanced
namespacePrefix.description: When marshalling using JAXB or SOAP then the JAXB implementation will automatic assign namespace prefixes. To control this mapping, Camel allows you to refer to a map which contains the desired mapping.

schema.description: To validate against an existing schema. You can use the prefix classpath:, file: or http: to specify how the resource should be resolved. You can separate multiple schema files by using the ',' character.

ignoreUnmarshalledHeaders.label: advanced
ignoreUnmarshalledHeaders.description: Whether to ignore headers that were not unmarshalled.
